questions.text: Two long, parallel conductors which are free to move are arranged 1.0 cm apart. A steady current of 20 A flows in each of the conductors in the same direction. The conductors:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Remains stationary: This would only be true if there was no current or if the currents were flowing in opposite directions.
When two parallel wires carry current in the same direction, they exert equal and opposite attractive forces on each other. This causes them to move towards one another.
Move away from each other: This would happen if the currents were flowing in opposite directions, creating a repulsive force.

options.text: Move at right angles to each other
options.explanation: Move at right angles to each other: The force between the conductors is either attractive or repulsive, acting along the line connecting them, not at right angles.

questions.explanation: When two parallel wires carry current in the same direction, they exert equal and opposite attractive forces on each other. This causes them to move towards one another.

questions.text: An electron enters in a magnetic field at an angle less than 90 degrees with magnetic field. Which of the following path is followed by an electron?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: An electron would follow a straight path if its velocity is completely parallel to the magnetic field lines (θ=0 or θ=180). In this case, there is no perpendicular component of velocity to cause circular or helical motion.

An electron will follow a circular path when it enters the magnetic field at an angle of 90 degrees to the field lines. This is because the entire velocity is perpendicular to the field, creating a centripetal force that results in circular motion.

A curved path is not a complete description. While the electron initially follows a curved trajectory when entering the field, it ultimately forms a helical path due to the combination of circular and linear motion.

When an electron enters a magnetic field at an angle between 0 and 90 degrees, its motion is helical. This is due to the presence of both perpendicular and parallel components of velocity relative to the magnetic field. The perpendicular component leads to circular motion, while the parallel component allows for linear motion, resulting in a helical path.

questions.explanation: When an electron enters a magnetic field at an angle less than 90 degrees, its velocity can be decomposed into components: one parallel and one perpendicular to the magnetic field. The perpendicular component of the velocity causes the electron to move in a circular path due to the Lorentz force, while the parallel component causes it to move linearly along the direction of the magnetic field. The combination of these two motions results in a helical path.The option 'Straight' is incorrect because it applies only when the velocity is completely parallel to the magnetic field. 'Circular' is incorrect as this occurs only when the velocity is entirely perpendicular to the field. 'Curve' is partially correct initially but does not describe the continuous motion, which is helical.

options.text: Nature of the material of the conducting wire

The direction of the magnetic lines of force does not depend upon the Area of the conducting wire.
The direction of the magnetic lines of force does not depend upon the Amount of the current.
The direction of the magnetic force depends on the direction of the current and the direction of the magnetic field.

questions.explanation: The direction of the magnetic force depends on the direction of the current and the direction of the magnetic field. Of these two, the direction of the current is in options so that will be the correct answer.

questions.text: Why picture on a TV screen become distorted when a magnet is brought near the screen?

options.text: Beam of electrons will be negative due to magnetic field.
options.explanation: Beam of electrons is already negative due to negative charge on electrons and not due to magnetic field.

options.text: Beam of electrons will be zero due to magnetic field.
options.explanation: Beam of electrons cannot become zero due to the magnetic field.

options.text: Beam of electrons will be reflected due to magnetic field.
options.explanation: C is wrong as electrons are not reflected due to magnetic field because reflection means to bounce off an objec, rather they are deflected i.e. their direction is changed.

options.text: Beam of electrons will be deflected due to magnetic field.
options.explanation: TVs basically work by releasing beams of electrons on a screen giving us the picture. However, bringing a magnet near a TV would form a magnetic field which would influence the beam of electrons changing their direction of motion.

questions.explanation: TVs basically work by releasing beams of electrons on a screen giving us the picture. However, bringing a magnet near a TV would form a magnetic field which would influence the beam of electrons changing their direction of motion.

questions.text: If a wire having current 10A has a 3T magnetic field, what will be the magnetic field at a point from the wire at double the distance?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Any current-carrying wire is surrounded by the magnetic field which is: B = µ0I/2πr It can be seen from the above equation that the magnetic field is inversely proportional to the distance and directly proportional to the current. When the distance from the wire becomes double, the magnetic field will become half, which is 1.5 T.
When distance from the wire becomes double, the magnetic field will become half, which is 1.5 T.
When the distance from the wire becomes double, the magnetic field will become half, which is 1.5 T.
When the distance from the wire becomes double, the magnetic field will become half, which is 1.5 T.

questions.explanation: Any current-carrying wire is surrounded by the magnetic field which is B = µ0I/2πr. It can be seen from the above equation that the magnetic field is inversely proportional to the distance and directly proportional to the current. When the distance from the wire becomes double, the magnetic field will reduce by a factor of two, which is 1.5 T.

questions.text: Is it possible to separate north pole only from bar magnet?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Option A is incorrect because you cannot separate only the north pole from a bar magnet. A bar magnet has two poles, north and south, and they cannot be isolated from each other.

Magnetism is a property that is caused due to separation of poles at atomic level. Even when the north pole is separated from the south pole, a new north pole is formed. So, we cannot separate the north pole from a bar magnet. Hence, option B is the correct option.

options.text: In some cases it is possible
options.explanation: Option C is incorrect because in all cases, you cannot separate only the north pole from a bar magnet. The magnet's poles are an intrinsic property, and they always come together as a pair.
This option is also not correct.

questions.explanation: Magnetism is a property that is caused due to separation of poles at atomic level. Even when the north pole is separated from the south pole, a new north pole is formed. So, we cannot separate the north pole from a bar magnet. Hence, option B is the correct option.

questions.text: When a charged particle is projected perpendicularly in a magnetic field its trajectory is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: A hyperbola is a curve that has two distinct branches and is not a closed shape. The trajectory of a charged particle projected perpendicularly into a magnetic field does not follow a hyperbola.

A helix is a three-dimensional curve characterized by a circular path combined with a linear motion along an axis. While the particle's motion can have a component in the direction of the magnetic field, the trajectory is not a helix because the particle does not exhibit a linear motion along an axis.
A parabola is a U-shaped curve. The trajectory of a charged particle projected perpendicularly into a magnetic field is not a parabola since it does not follow a U-shaped path.
The correct answer circular because a charged particle projected perpendicularly into a magnetic field moves in a circular trajectory due to the perpendicular Lorentz force acting on it.

questions.explanation: If a charged particle is projected perpendicular to a uniform magnetic field, then the particle revolves in a circular path and its kinetic energy remains constant since there is no work done by magnetic force. Since a circular path direction of a particle changes, momentum also changes.

questions.text: A force which is experienced in a magnetic field depends on:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The force depends on the magnitude of the charge, as described in the formula F = qvB sin(θ).

options.text: speed of the moving charge v
options.explanation: The force depends on the velocity of the charge, as indicated in the formula F = qvB sin(θ).
The force depends on the magnetic field strength, as given by the formula F = qvB sin(θ).

The magnetic force on a charge is determined by the product of the charge magnitude, its velocity, and the magnetic field strength, as expressed in F = qvB sin(θ). Therefore, it depends on all these factors.
This is incorrect as the force does depend on the charge, velocity, and magnetic field strength, as per the formula F = qvB sin(θ).

questions.explanation: The force experienced by a charge moving in a magnetic field is given by the formula F = qvB sin(θ), where q is the charge, v is the velocity of the charge, and B is the magnetic field strength. The angle θ is the angle between the velocity vector and the magnetic field lines. Therefore, the force depends on the magnitude of the charge, the speed of the moving charge, and the strength of the magnetic field. Option D correctly identifies all these factors. Options A, B, and C each identify only one of these factors, which is insufficient on its own. Option E is incorrect as it suggests that the force depends on none of these factors, which is contrary to the established formula.

questions.text: A conductor carrying a current 'I' has length 'L'. When it is placed in a magnetic field 'B' at 90°, it experiences a force:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is correct. The force on a conductor carrying current 'I' in a magnetic field 'B' at an angle of 90° is given by the formula F = BILsin(θ). Since the angle is 90°, sin(90°) = 1, making the force BIL.
This is incorrect. The formula for force does not involve squaring the magnetic field 'B'. The correct formula is F = BILsin(θ), not B²LI.
This is incorrect. The current 'I' should not be squared in the formula for the force. The correct formula is F = BILsin(θ), not BLI².
This is incorrect. The force is zero if the angle is 0° or 180°, but here the angle is 90°, which results in a maximum force.
This is incorrect. The force is finite and given by the formula F = BILsin(θ). It cannot be infinite.

questions.explanation: The formula for the force experienced by a current-carrying conductor is BILsin(θ), where theta represents the angle between the current, I, and the magnetic field, B, hence sin(90)=1 and the value of the force will be BIL (maximum).

questions.text: Two parallel straight conductors, in which current is flowing in the same direction, attract each other. The cause of it is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Two parallel straight conductors carrying current in the same direction attract each other due to the magnetic force between them. This happens because:Magnetic Fields Around the Conductors:Each current-carrying conductor generates a circular magnetic field around it, as described by Ampère's right-hand rule.Magnetic Interaction:The magnetic field of one conductor exerts a force on the second conductor and vice versa.When currents are in the same direction, the magnetic fields between the wires reinforce each other in a way that causes an attractive force.Lorentz Force Explanation:The moving charges (current) in one conductor experience a force due to the magnetic field created by the other conductor.According to the Lorentz force law, this force acts inwards, pulling the conductors together.
Two parallel straight conductors carrying current in the same direction attract each other due to the magnetic force between them.
Two parallel straight conductors carrying current in the same direction attract each other due to the magnetic force between them.
Two parallel straight conductors carrying current in the same direction attract each other due to the magnetic force between them.

questions.explanation: Correct option is 'A'.

questions.text: Magnetic flux is maximum when angle between magnetic field and vector area is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: At an angle of 0 degrees, the magnetic field lines are perfectly aligned with the area vector, resulting in maximum magnetic flux. This is because cos(0) = 1, maximizing the product of the magnetic induction and the area.
At an angle of 90 degrees, the magnetic field lines are perpendicular to the area vector, resulting in zero magnetic flux. This is because cos(90) = 0, meaning no flux passes through the area.

At an angle of 180 degrees, the magnetic field lines are again perfectly aligned, but in the opposite direction. This also results in maximum magnetic flux as cos(180) = -1, indicating that the direction of the flux is reversed but the magnitude remains the same as at 0 degrees.

This option is correct because both 0 degrees and 180 degrees yield maximum (but opposite direction) magnetic flux, as explained above. However, it is essential to note that while both angles provide maximum flux, they represent different directions of the magnetic field.

questions.explanation: Magnetic flux is calculated as the product of the magnetic field strength (B) and the area (ΔA) through which it passes, adjusted by the cosine of the angle (θ) between the magnetic field lines and the normal (perpendicular) to the area vector. The formula is given by: Flux = BΔA cos(θ). The cosine function reaches its maximum value of 1 at an angle of 0 degrees, indicating that the magnetic field is aligned with the area vector. However, at 180 degrees, the magnetic field is aligned in the opposite direction, leading to a maximum absolute value of flux, but with a negative sign due to the cosine function. Therefore, the correct answers are 0 degrees and 180 degrees, making Option A correct. Options B and D are incorrect because 90 degrees results in zero flux, and while Option C is correct, it does not account for the nature of the question's request for a single maximum angle. Hence, while A and C are maximized in terms of magnitude, they represent different directional scenarios.

questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Magnetic field and speed of the charge only

questions.text: The SI unit of magnetic flux is Weber which is equivalent to:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The unit NA/m is the unit of magnetic field strength, also known as magnetic field intensity or magnetic field intensity. It represents the SI unit for magnetic field strength and is measured in amperes per meter (A/m).

The unit NmA is not a recognized unit in the SI system. Newton-meter (Nm) is the SI unit of torque or moment of force, representing the product of a force (measured in newtons) and the perpendicular distance from the axis of rotation (measured in meters). Milliampere (mA) is the SI unit for electrical current, representing one thousandth of an ampere. Combining the two units, NmA, would not have a specific meaning or representation in a recognized SI unit.

The unit Nm^-1A^-1 represents the reciprocal of the unit for electrical resistance, which is ohm (Ω). Reciprocal of ohm: (Ω)^-1 = Ω^-1 = Nm^-1A^-1 Therefore, Nm^-1A^-1 is the unit of electrical conductance, which is the measure of how easily electric current can flow through a material. It is commonly denoted by the unit siemens (S) in the SI system.

The SI unit of magnetic flux is indeed the Weber (Wb). The Weber is defined as the amount of magnetic flux that passes through a surface of one square meter (1 m²) perpendicular to a magnetic field of one Tesla (1 T). In terms of base SI units, one Weber is equivalent to one Tesla meter squared (T·m²).

questions.explanation: The formula for the magnetic field density (B) is: B=F/IL {where F is the force, I is the current and L is the length of the conductor.} Substitute the SI units into the equation: B=N/Am The magnetic flux is BA therefore: B=N/Am x m2 =NmA-1

questions.text: A particle having mass m and charge q moves in a circular path in a magnetic field of strength B. If the radius of the circular path is r, the time required by this particle to complete one revolution is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The time required for a particle to complete one revolution in a circular path of radius r can be calculated using the formula: T = 2πr/v where v is the velocity of the particle. In a magnetic field of strength B, the force on a charged particle q with velocity v is given by F = qvB. The force provides the necessary centripetal force mv²/r, so we have: qvB = mv²/r Solving for v, we get: v = (qBr/m) Substituting this value of v in the formula for T, we get: T = 2πr/v = 2πm/qB Therefore, the time required by the particle to complete one revolution is 2πm/qB. Answer A is correct.
This formula is incorrect because it erroneously includes the radius r in the denominator, which is not present in the correct formula for the period.
This expression represents the angular frequency, not the period. The period should be expressed as the reciprocal of the angular frequency, which is not the case here.
This formula incorrectly includes an extra factor of q in the numerator, which does not align with the correct formula for the period.
This formula omits the necessary factor of π, which is essential in the formula for the period of circular motion in a magnetic field.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A: 2πm/qB. When a charged particle moves in a magnetic field, the magnetic force acts as the centripetal force required for circular motion. The force on the particle is given by qvB, which equals the centripetal force mv²/r. By equating and solving for the velocity v, we find v = qBr/m. Substituting this into the formula for the period, T = 2πr/v, results in T = 2πm/qB.Option B includes an unnecessary factor of r in the denominator, Option C represents angular frequency instead of the period, Option D incorrectly includes an extra factor of q in the numerator, and Option E omits the necessary factor of π.

questions.text: The magnetic lines of force are directed in a manner that they:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Originate at south pole and terminate at north pole
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The magnetic lines of force originate at the north pole and terminate at the south pole.

This is correct. The magnetic lines of force are continuous, so they must pass through the magnet. They do not pass through the magnet in a straight line, but rather they form loops that go around the magnet.

options.text: Originate at north pole and terminate at south pole
options.explanation: The magnetic lines of force are directed in a manner that they originate at the north pole and terminate at the south pole. This is because the north pole of a magnet is a region where the magnetic field lines are pointing outwards, and the south pole of a magnet is a region where the magnetic field lines are pointing inwards.The magnetic lines of force are continuous, so they must pass through the magnet. They do not pass through the magnet in a straight line, but rather they form loops that go around the magnet.The magnetic lines of force do not go away from both the poles. If they did, then the magnetic field would be zero everywhere outside the magnet. However, the magnetic field is not zero outside the magnet. It is strongest near the poles of the magnet, and it decreases as you move away from the poles.

options.text: Go away from both the poles
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The magnetic lines of force do not go away from both the poles. If they did, then the magnetic field would be zero everywhere outside the magnet. However, the magnetic field is not zero outside the magnet. It is strongest near the poles of the magnet, and it decreases as you move away from the poles.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C: The magnetic lines of force originate at the north pole and terminate at the south pole. This is because magnetic field lines emerge from the north pole and enter the south pole, completing a loop.Option A is incorrect because it reverses the direction of the magnetic lines of force, which do not originate at the south pole.Option B is incorrect in context because, while magnetic lines do pass through the magnet as continuous loops, it does not address their directional origin and termination.Option D is incorrect because magnetic lines of force do not move away from both poles; they form loops, ensuring the magnetic field is present outside the magnet.

questions.text: The magnetic field is produced in a solenoid depends on:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The length of the solenoid does affect the magnetic field, but it is not as significant as the number of turns and current. The magnetic field is strongest at the center of the solenoid, and it decreases as you move away from the center. The length of the solenoid determines how far the magnetic field extends from the center.

options.text: Its length and current in it
options.explanation: The current flowing through the solenoid also affects the magnetic field. The stronger the current, the stronger the magnetic field will be. This is because the current creates a magnetic field around the wire, and the more current there is, the stronger the magnetic field will be.

options.text: Its length and number of turns in it
options.explanation: The number of turns in the solenoid also affects the magnetic field. The more turns there are, the stronger the magnetic field will be. This is because each turn of the solenoid creates a magnetic field, and the more turns there are, the stronger the combined magnetic field will be.

options.text: The number of turns and current in it
options.explanation: The magnetic field strength in a solenoid is directly proportional to both the number of turns and the current flowing through it. These are the primary factors that determine the field's magnitude.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is The number of turns and current in it. The magnetic field strength inside a solenoid is determined by the product of the current flowing through it and the number of turns per unit length. While the length of the solenoid influences the uniformity of the magnetic field, it does not significantly impact its strength. Thus, Option D is correct. Options A, B, and C incorrectly emphasize the length of the solenoid as a primary factor, which is not the case.

questions.text: Complete the magnetic flux formula: Cos Θ = Φ/?
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: We know that the formula for magnetic flux is:Φ = BAcosθwhere θ is the angle between a perpendicular vector to the area and the magnetic field, A is the area and B is the magnetic flux densityRearranging this formula gives:cosθ = Φ/BA

questions.text: Face of coil having clockwise current:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: If the current flows in an anti-clockwise direction on the face of the coil, it behaves like a north pole according to the clock face rule. However, since the current is clockwise, this option is incorrect.
The correct answer. When current flows in a clockwise direction on the face of the coil, it behaves like a south pole, as per the clock face rule.

options.text: Becomes a magnet of varying poles
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because the polarity of a coil does not vary with time; it is determined by the direction of the current in the coil.

options.text: Does not behave like a magnet
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because a current-carrying coil always behaves as a magnet, forming an electromagnet with defined north and south poles.

questions.explanation: A current-carrying loop or coil functions as an electromagnet. The direction of current flow determines the polarity of the magnetic field produced. Using the clock face rule: if you look at the face of the coil with current flowing in a clockwise direction, it behaves like a south pole. Conversely, if the current flows anti-clockwise, it behaves like a north pole. Therefore, the correct answer is that the face of the coil with a clockwise current behaves like the south pole.Option A is incorrect because clockwise current results in a south pole, not a north pole. Option C is incorrect because the polarity does not vary; it remains constant as long as the current direction is constant. Option D is incorrect because a current-carrying coil always behaves as a magnet, thus possessing magnetic properties with defined poles.

questions.text: If we make the magnetic field stronger, the value of induced current is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: If the magnetic field strength increases, the induced current cannot decrease.

The induced current in a conductor is given by Faraday’s Law of Electromagnetic Induction:E=-Nφ/tIf we increase the magnetic field strength , the rate of change of flux increases, leading to a greater induced EMF, and hence more induced current (assuming resistance remains constant).
The induced current does not vanish unless there is no change in flux or the circuit is open.
Since EMF depends on , increasing will change the induced current, so it cannot remain constant.

questions.explanation: Faraday's Law says that the emf induced (and therefore the current induced) in the loop is proportional to the rate of change in magnetic flux and therefore to magnetic flux.We know that magnetic flux = BAcos θWhere B is the magnetic flux density, A is the area and θ is the angle between a perpendicular vector to the area and the magnetic field.Thus, Induced emf (and therefore the induced current) is also proportional to magnetic field strength/magnetic flux density.

Since none or a similar option is not explicitly listed, I assume All cases are not possible is closest, implying no direct visualization method among the choices. questions.text: Under what condition the force acting on charge particle moving in the magnetic field minimum?
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: If it is moving parallel to the magnetic field.
options.explanation: When a charged particle moves parallel to the magnetic field lines, the angle is 0 degrees, and the sine of 0 is 0, resulting in no force.

options.text: If it is moving antiparallel to the magnetic field.
options.explanation: When a charged particle moves antiparallel to the magnetic field lines, the angle is 180 degrees, and the sine of 180 is 0, resulting in no force.

options.text: If the charge particle moves perpendicular to the magnetic field.
options.explanation: When the charge moves perpendicular to the magnetic field, the angle is 90 degrees, and the sine of 90 is 1, resulting in maximum force, not minimum.
Both moving parallel and antiparallel to the magnetic field results in zero force, as the sine of the angle (0 or 180 degrees) is zero.

questions.explanation: The magnetic force on a moving charge is given by the equation F = qvB sin(θ), where θ is the angle between the velocity vector and the magnetic field. The force is minimum (zero) when the angle θ is 0 degrees (parallel) or 180 degrees (antiparallel), as sin(0) = sin(180) = 0. Thus, Options A, B, and D are correct. Option C is incorrect because the force is maximized when the angle is 90 degrees, not minimized.

questions.text: The unit of magnetic flux density is the tesla, ‘T’. It can also be expressed as:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: For this option, the units do not align with the definition of tesla. Recall that Tesla = (Newton)/(Ampere x metre). Hence, this option is incorrect as it lacks the correct dimensional analysis.
This option implies an incorrect relationship as the inverse unit structure does not correspond with the unit of tesla, which is derived as (Newton)/(Ampere x metre).
This unit structure is incorrect for tesla as it not only involves inverses but also lacks the appropriate dimensional analysis to match with the tesla definition.
This is the correct option. It aligns with the definition of tesla, which is derived from the equation F = BIL sin(θ). Here, Tesla = (Newton)/(Ampere x metre).

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D: 1 N A-1 m-1. This is because the tesla is defined as the magnetic flux density that, when applied perpendicular to a conductor carrying a current of one ampere, results in a force of one newton per meter of the conductor. Thus, 1 T = 1 N/(A·m).Other options fail to represent this relationship. Option A lacks the correct dimensional analysis by missing the inverse meter. Option B and Option C involve inverse units that do not correspond to the derived unit for tesla.

questions.text: If a charge of 2C is moving with 5m/s enter at 30° in 3 T, calculate the force experienced by it.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: To calculate the force experienced by a charge moving through a magnetic field, you can use the formula for the magnetic force on a charged particle:F = q * v * B * sin(theta)Where:F = Force experienced by the charge (in Newtons)q = Charge of the particle (in Coulombs)v = Velocity of the particle (in meters per second)B = Magnetic field strength (in Tesla)theta = Angle between the velocity vector and the magnetic field vector (in degrees)Given:q = 2 C (Coulombs)v = 5 m/s (meters per second)B = 3 T (Tesla)theta = 30°First, we need to convert the angle from degrees to radians:theta_rad = 30° * (π / 180°) ≈ 0.5236 radiansNow, we can calculate the force:F = 2 C * 5 m/s * 3 T * sin(0.5236 radians)F ≈ 2 * 5 * 3 * 0.5F ≈ 15 NThe force experienced by the charge is approximately 15 Newtons.

To calculate the force experienced by a charge moving through a magnetic field, you can use the formula for the magnetic force on a charged particle:F = q * v * B * sin(theta)Where:F = Force experienced by the charge (in Newtons)q = Charge of the particle (in Coulombs)v = Velocity of the particle (in meters per second)B = Magnetic field strength (in Tesla)theta = Angle between the velocity vector and the magnetic field vector (in degrees)Given:q = 2 C (Coulombs)v = 5 m/s (meters per second)B = 3 T (Tesla)theta = 30°First, we need to convert the angle from degrees to radians:theta_rad = 30° * (π / 180°) ≈ 0.5236 radiansNow, we can calculate the force:F = 2 C * 5 m/s * 3 T * sin(0.5236 radians)F ≈ 2 * 5 * 3 * 0.5F ≈ 15 NThe force experienced by the charge is approximately 15 Newtons.

To calculate the force experienced by a charge moving through a magnetic field, you can use the formula for the magnetic force on a charged particle:F = q * v * B * sin(theta)Where:F = Force experienced by the charge (in Newtons)q = Charge of the particle (in Coulombs)v = Velocity of the particle (in meters per second)B = Magnetic field strength (in Tesla)theta = Angle between the velocity vector and the magnetic field vector (in degrees)Given:q = 2 C (Coulombs)v = 5 m/s (meters per second)B = 3 T (Tesla)theta = 30°First, we need to convert the angle from degrees to radians:theta_rad = 30° * (π / 180°) ≈ 0.5236 radiansNow, we can calculate the force:F = 2 C * 5 m/s * 3 T * sin(0.5236 radians)F ≈ 2 * 5 * 3 * 0.5F ≈ 15 NThe force experienced by the charge is approximately 15 Newtons.

questions.explanation: q x v x b x sin(θ)2 x 5 x 3 x sin(30)=15

questions.text: Which, among the following qualities, is not affected by the magnetic field?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: A moving charge is affected by the magnetic field due to the magnetic force experienced by the charge when it moves through the magnetic field.
The magnetic field indirectly affects the charge in magnetic flux through the factors determining the magnetic flux.

When an electric current flows through a conductor, it generates a magnetic field around the conductor. This is described by Ampere's law. Therefore, the magnetic field it produces affects the current flowing in a conductor.

A magnetic field does not affect a stationary charge because it has no velocity. We know that force on a charged particle due to a magnetic field = Bvqsinθ. When v = 0, the force due to the magnetic field on the charged particle will be 0 and the magnetic field will not affect the charged particle.

questions.explanation: A magnetic field does not affect a stationary charge because it has no velocity. We know that force on a charged particle due to a magnetic field = Bvqsinθ. When v = 0, the force due to the magnetic field on the charged particle will be 0 and the magnetic field will not affect the charged particle.

questions.text: The strength of a magnetic field is called?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Strength is not correct because it is a general term and does not specifically refer to the strength of a magnetic field.
Flux is not correct because it refers to the total amount of magnetic field passing through a given area, which depends on both the strength and the size of the magnetic field.

Magnetic flux density (B) is defined as the force acting per unit current per unit length on a wire placed at right angles to the magnetic field. Magnetic field strength is also called Magnetic Intensity or Magnetic Field Intensity or Magnetic Field Strength.
Density is not correct because it is a general term that refers to the amount of a substance or quantity per unit volume or area, and does not specifically refer to the strength of a magnetic field.

questions.explanation: .Option A) Strength is not correct because it is a general term and does not specifically refer to the strength of a magnetic field.Option B) Flux is not correct because it refers to the total amount of magnetic field passing through a given area, which depends on both the strength and the size of the magnetic field.Option C) Magnetic flux density (B) is defined as the force acting per unit current per unit length on a wire placed at right angles to the magnetic field. Magnetic field strength is also called Magnetic Intensity or Magnetic Field Intensity or Magnetic Field Strength. Option D) Density is not correct because it is a general term that refers to the amount of a substance or quantity per unit volume or area and does not specifically refer to the strength of a magnetic field.

questions.text: Magnetic flux is a?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Magnetic flux is a scalar quantity.
A dot product does not give a vector quantity. Hence, this option is incorrect.
A quantity cannot be sometimes vector and sometimes scalar. It is an intrinsic property, and one quantity can only be one of these. Hence, this option is incorrect.
As option A is correct, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Magnetic flux ΦB = BAcosOr it is a dot product, so we may say thatΦB = B.AWhere B = vector for magnetic field, and A = vector for area.The answer of a dot product is always a scalar. Hence, A option is correct.

questions.text: The flux is the region where magnetic field:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This is incorrect. Magnetic flux does not specifically depend on changes in the direction of the magnetic field, but rather on how many field lines pass through a surface.
This option is incorrect. While changes in field strength can affect the flux, flux itself is not defined by changes in strength, but by the number of field lines through a surface.
This is the incorrect option. Magnetic flux is not concerned with polarity changes; it involves the total number of magnetic field lines through a surface.
This option is correct because magnetic flux itself is a measure of the number of magnetic field lines through a surface area, irrespective of changes in the field.

questions.explanation: Magnetic flux is defined as the number of magnetic field lines passing through a given surface area. It is a measure of the magnetic field's presence and intensity in a specific region. The correct answer is that the flux is the region where the magnetic field does not change because the definition of magnetic flux is static; it does not imply a change in direction, strength, or polarity. Option D is correct because it aligns with the fundamental definition of magnetic flux. The other options incorrectly associate flux with changes in direction, strength, or polarity, which do not define the static nature of magnetic flux.

questions.text: Magnetic flux is the scalar product of:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: this is not a correct option.A. B and V: 'V' typically represents velocity. The scalar product of the magnetic field and velocity is not magnetic flux.
Magnetic Flux = BAcosθ
this is not a correct option. C. B and I: 'I' typically represents current. The scalar product of the magnetic field and current is not magnetic flux.
this is not a correct option. D. None of these: As explained above, the correct answer is B and A.

questions.explanation: Magnetic Flux = BAcosθ

questions.text: Magnetic field lines have a property that lines are _.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option suggests that magnetic field lines do not intersect each other. In reality, magnetic field lines do not intersect because they represent the path of the magnetic field around a magnetic object. Magnetic field lines always form closed loops, and they do not cross each other.

This option suggests that magnetic field lines intersect near the south pole of a magnet. However, this is not correct. Magnetic field lines emerge from the north pole of a magnet and re-enter at the south pole, forming closed loops. They do not intersect each other at the south pole or anywhere else.

This option suggests that magnetic field lines intersect near the north pole of a magnet. Similar to option (b), this is not correct. Magnetic field lines do not intersect each other at the north pole or anywhere else. Instead, they emerge from the north pole and re-enter at the south pole.

This option suggests that magnetic field lines intersect at every point around a magnet. However, this is not correct. Magnetic field lines never intersect each other. They always form closed loops, and they do not cross.

questions.explanation: The magnetic field lines do not intersect, under normal conditions, without any external influences.

questions.text: Moving charges have:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Electric field and magnetic field both
options.explanation: A moving charge generates an electric field due to its charge and a magnetic field due to its motion. Thus, it has both fields.
This is incorrect because moving charges do produce fields. A moving charge cannot have no fields at all.
This is incorrect because, while a moving charge does produce a magnetic field, it also inherently has an electric field due to its charge.
This is incorrect because a moving charge generates a magnetic field in addition to its electric field.

questions.explanation: A moving charge generates both an electric field and a magnetic field. The electric field is due to the charge itself, as any charged particle will create an electric field in its surroundings. When the charge is in motion, it also generates a magnetic field, as moving charges are a source of magnetic fields according to electromagnetic theory. Therefore, the correct answer is that moving charges have both electric and magnetic fields.

questions.text: The magnetic field inside a solenoid is:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: Inside a solenoid, the magnetic field lines are parallel and very close to each other. Therefore, the magnetic field inside a solenoid is uniform and maximum.

questions.text: The value of permeability of free space is:
questions.subject: Physics
questions.explanation: The permeability of free space, μ0, is a physical constant used often in electromagnetism. Its value is 4π x 10-7 N/Am (newtons per ampere squared).

questions.text: Tesla is the unit of:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect
Incorrect

The SI unit of magnetic flux density or magnetic field strength is Tesla (T). One tesla is the magnetic flux density that exerts a force of one newton on a 1 m wire carrying a current of 1 A placed perpendicularly to the magnetic field.Ohm is the SI unit of resistivity.Henry is the SI unit of inductance.V/m is the unit of electric field strength.
Incorrect

questions.explanation: The SI unit of magnetic flux density or magnetic field strength is Tesla (T). One tesla is the magnetic flux density that exerts a force of one newton on a 1 m wire carrying a current of 1 A placed perpendicularly to the magnetic field.Ohm is the SI unit of resistivity.Henry is the SI unit of inductance.V/m is the unit of electric field strength.

questions.text: In F = ILB sin θ, θ is the angle between:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The angle θ is not between the force F and the current I. The formula relates θ to the orientation of the conductor and the magnetic field, not the force and current.
The angle θ does not pertain to the force F and the length L of the conductor. The formula uses θ to measure the relative orientation of the conductor and the magnetic field.
Correct. The angle θ in the formula F = ILB sin θ represents the angle between the direction of the length L of the conductor and the magnetic field B.
The angle θ is not defined between the current I and the length L of the conductor in this context. It is concerned with the orientation of the conductor relative to the magnetic field B.

questions.explanation: The formula F = ILB sin θ describes the magnetic force on a current-carrying conductor in a uniform magnetic field. Here, θ is the angle between the direction of the conductor (L) and the magnetic field (B). When L is perpendicular to B (θ = 90°), the sin 90° = 1, resulting in maximum force: F = ILB. If L is parallel to B (θ = 0°), then sin 0° = 0, and the force is zero: F = 0.Option A is incorrect because θ is not the angle between the force and the current. Option B is incorrect because θ is not the angle between the force and the length of the conductor. Option D is incorrect because θ is not the angle between the current and the length of the conductor.

questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Fc = Fmmv^2/r = BevSimplifying and rearranging, we gete/m = v/Br

questions.explanation: Fc = Fmmv^2/r = BevSimplifying and rearranging, we gete/m = v/Br

questions.text: Choose the sentence that is grammatically correct:
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: The sentence "He weighed himself two pounds." is incorrect because it implies that the person is weighing himself, but the measurement given ("two pounds") would typically refer to an object or a different context. The reflexive pronoun "himself" is not needed in this context
The correct sentence is:He weighed two pounds. This sentence is correct because it indicates the weight of the person or object being referred to.
This is incorrect because there is no subject-pronoun agreement present. The subject- he and the pronoun- themselves do not agree
This is also incorrect because this iption also lacks subject-pronoun agreement.

questions.explanation: Option A is correct as it shows correct subject-pronoun agreement.

questions.text: Choose the correct option.We felt as if the ground was _ beneath our feet.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: The word digging means to make a hole or remove soil using hands or tools.In this sentence, it doesn’t make sense because the ground itself cannot “dig.”It implies an active process done by a person or machine, not something you feel.The phrase “ground digging beneath our feet” sounds unnatural and confusing.It doesn’t convey the idea of instability or movement.Therefore, this word doesn’t fit the emotional or physical sense of the sentence.Hence, digging is not the correct choice here.

The word sinking means going down or being swallowed by something like water or sand.It could slightly fit if the sentence described the ground collapsing or moving downward.However, the expression “felt as if the ground was sinking” implies gradual descent, not sudden instability.It doesn’t match the idiomatic sense of losing balance or stability.“Sinking” conveys heaviness or hopelessness rather than slipping or shaking.Thus, it doesn’t fully capture the intended feeling of unease.So, sinking is not the most accurate word here.

The word slipping perfectly fits the context of this expression.It gives the feeling that the ground is moving or becoming unstable beneath your feet.This suggests uncertainty, fear, or loss of control — both physically and emotionally.It’s a common idiomatic phrase used to describe instability or insecurity in life.For example, when situations change suddenly, people say, “The ground is slipping beneath our feet.”It paints a vivid image of losing footing or confidence.Hence, slipping is the correct and most appropriate option.

The word bursting means exploding, breaking open, or suddenly releasing pressure.It doesn’t make sense in the phrase “the ground was bursting beneath our feet.”While it could describe an earthquake or eruption, the sentence here expresses feeling, not action.The idiomatic tone of the sentence calls for instability, not destruction.“Bursting” feels too violent and literal for this emotional expression.It suggests an outward explosion rather than a subtle movement.Thus, bursting is incorrect in both meaning and usage.

questions.explanation: This is an informal expression that means feeling unstable or losing one’s footing. It’s often used metaphorically to describe a situation where things are unpredictable or uncertain. In the context of the sentence, it conveys the idea that the ground is not stable beneath our feet.

questions.text: A few years ago, it _ that human beings do not belong to this earth.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: The word found is the simple past form of find, meaning to discover something.However, using found alone here makes the sentence grammatically incomplete.“It found that…” is incorrect because “it” cannot perform the action of finding in this context.The sentence needs a passive construction since the subject “it” receives the action.Also, found alone implies an active voice, which doesn’t suit the meaning here.We are talking about something that was discovered, not something that did the discovering.Therefore, found is not the correct choice.

The phrase was found is in the past tense and passive voice, which perfectly fits this sentence.The subject “it” is singular, so the singular verb “was” agrees correctly.The time phrase “a few years ago” clearly indicates past tense.Thus, “it was found” means “it was discovered” or “it came to be known.”This makes the sentence smooth and meaningful: “A few years ago, it was found that human beings do not belong to this earth.”The structure is grammatically correct and conveys a sense of revelation or discovery.Hence, was found is the correct and appropriate option.

The phrase were found is also in the past tense but plural in form.Since the subject “it” is singular, were cannot be used here.Using were would make the sentence grammatically incorrect.The helping verb must agree with the singular subject “it.”For example, we say “it was found,” not “it were found.”Although were found works with plural subjects (“they were found”), it does not fit this case.Therefore, were found is not correct here.

The phrase had found is in the past perfect tense and indicates an action completed before another past event.However, there’s no second past event mentioned in this sentence.Using had found here creates confusion and incorrect tense sequencing.Also, “it had found” implies that it actively discovered something, which isn’t logical in this context.If we wanted to use the past perfect in passive voice, it should be “had been found.”Since that’s not given, had found remains grammatically wrong and incomplete.Hence, had found is not the correct choice.

questions.explanation: In the given statement, "A few years ago" represents that the sentence is in past tense, and "it" represents that helping verb should be singular. Option D is incorrect because if we used had, it should should have been followed by "been". Hence, correct option is B.

questions.text: A shoal of fish _ killed by the fishermen.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: Correct, because "shoal" is a singular noun (referring to a group of fish treated as a single entity), so the singular form "has been" is used.
Incorrect, as "have" is used for plural subjects, but "shoal" is singular.
Incorrect, as "had" is the past perfect tense, which isn't needed here; the action is in the present perfect.
Incorrect without "been." While "has" might be used with singular subjects, it requires "been" to form the correct tense here (present perfect).

questions.explanation: Correct, because "shoal" is a singular noun (referring to a group of fish treated as a single entity), so the singular form "has been" is used.

questions.text: At last the fly was _. The most appropriate word to be filled in is:
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: Correct, as it suggests the fly was caught or ensnared by the spider, which is a common scenario in nature.
Incorrect, as this implies the fly is enjoying or amused by the spider, which doesn’t fit the context.
Incorrect, as it suggests the fly was intentionally welcomed by the spider, which is unrealistic.
Incorrect, as this would imply the spider let the fly go, which doesn’t match the typical predatory behavior of spiders.

questions.explanation: Trapped by the spider is correct, as it suggests the fly was caught or ensnared by the spider, which is a common scenario in nature.

questions.text: Choose the correct meaning of the underlined word;Providentially, his mind was not injured.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: The word slowly means at a low speed or without haste.It describes the pace or speed of an action, not the manner of fortune or luck.In this sentence, providentially refers to something happening by divine favor or good fortune.Using slowly would completely change the meaning and make the sentence illogical.It would suggest that his mind avoided injury because of slowness, which makes no sense.Thus, it doesn’t match the meaning of providentially.Therefore, slowly is an incorrect option.

The word suddenly means happening quickly or without warning.It describes a sudden action or change in time, not an event guided by fate or luck.“Providentially” suggests divine intervention or fortunate timing, not speed.If we say “suddenly, his mind was not injured,” it changes the meaning to something abrupt.This removes the sense of fortune or blessing from the sentence.Hence, suddenly does not carry the same meaning as providentially.So, suddenly is not the correct answer.

The word luckily means fortunately or by good luck — something positive happening by chance.It directly matches the meaning of providentially, which implies being saved or helped by divine or fortunate cause.Both words suggest that a good outcome happened unexpectedly.In the sentence, “Providentially, his mind was not injured,” it means he was fortunate to escape harm.Replacing it with luckily keeps the same meaning and tone intact.It conveys relief and gratitude for an unexpected good result.Therefore, luckily is the correct synonym for providentially.
This option is incorrect, as unfortunately means by bad luck or unluckily.Therefore, it can be an antonym of the given word, not the synonym.

questions.explanation: Some common synonyms of providentially are fortunately, happily, and luckily. While all these words mean "meeting with unforeseen success," providentially more definitely implies the help or intervention of a higher power.

questions.text: A carpenter carves _ on cab doors. Choose the right word to fill in the blank.
questions.subject: English

options.explanation: "Arabesque" refers to intricate, decorative patterns often used in woodwork and carvings, making it the most appropriate word for the kind of design a carpenter might carve on cab doors.
Ornate is an adjective meaning heavily decorated. Again, you can’t carve ornate — you can carve ornate patterns.
Decked means decorated or adorned, but it’s a verb/adjective, not a thing you carve. You can deck something, but you can’t carve a decked.
Floral is an adjective meaning flower-related, not a noun. You can carve floral designs, but not just floral.

questions.explanation: "Arabesque" refers to intricate, decorative patterns often used in woodwork and carvings, making it the most appropriate word for the kind of design a carpenter might carve on cab doors.Decked means decorated or adorned, but it’s a verb/adjective, not a thing you carve. You can deck something, but you can’t carve a decked.Ornate is an adjective meaning heavily decorated. Again, you can’t carve ornate — you can carve ornate patterns.Floral is an adjective meaning flower-related, not a noun. You can carve floral designs, but not just floral.

questions.text: Read the following statements and identify the best cause and effect relation.I. State P started providing multiple rehabilitation schemes, like free education, job search help and help groups for addicts, etc. over the past year.II. Crimes rates for state P have steadily decreased over the past year.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: II is the cause and I is its effect
options.explanation: This option suggests that the decrease in crime rates led to the introduction of rehabilitation schemes, which is illogical as typically, rehabilitation schemes are implemented to reduce crime rates, not as a result of decreased crime.

options.text: I is the cause and II is its effect
options.explanation: This option posits that the implementation of rehabilitation schemes directly caused the decrease in crime rates, which is a logical and plausible explanation.

options.text: I and II are effects of some common cause
options.explanation: This suggests that both the introduction of rehabilitation schemes and the decrease in crime rates stem from a common cause, which is possible but less likely given the direct relationship between rehabilitation and crime reduction.

options.text: I and II are both effects of independent causes
options.explanation: This implies that the rehabilitation schemes and the decrease in crime rates are unrelated and caused by separate factors, which is unlikely given the context of the statements.

questions.explanation: The best cause and effect relation in this scenario is: I is the cause and II is its effect. The implementation of rehabilitation schemes like free education, job search help, and help groups for addicts is likely aimed at reducing crime rates, making it a plausible direct cause of the observed decrease in crime rates in state P.Option A is incorrect because it suggests the opposite causal direction, which is illogical. Option C is less likely because the direct relationship between rehabilitation efforts and crime reduction is well-documented, making a common cause less plausible. Option D is incorrect as it suggests unrelated causes, which is inconsistent with the context provided.

questions.text: Read the following statement, assuming everything in it to be true. Then decide which of the given suggested courses of action logically follow and are worth pursuing.Statement: Your toddler drops and breaks a glass by genuine accident.Courses of Action:I. yell at them to be more careful with things made out of glass.II. Punish them to make sure they remember to be extra careful next time.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Yelling teaches fear, not learning. Toddlers don’t have the impulse control or the understanding to benefit from being shouted at; it usually makes them anxious and less likely to learn the intended lesson. Yelling can also harm trust and escalate the situation (crying, upset), so it’s not worth pursuing.

Punishment for an honest accident doesn’t reliably teach safer behaviour and can create shame or fear. Toddlers learn best from calm guidance, repetition, and safe practice. Punishment may make them hide future mistakes or become anxious, so it’s not an appropriate or effective course.

Combining yelling and punishment compounds the problems above: it still doesn’t teach the toddler why glass is dangerous or how to act differently, and it risks emotional harm. So both together are even less justified.

This is the best choice: respond calmly, keep everyone safe, and turn it into a simple learning moment.Quick, practical alternative steps you can take right away.Ensure safety first: remove any child from the area and clear up broken glass yourself (or while they watch from a safe distance).Comfort the child if they’re frightened; reassure them accidents happen.Give a very short, simple explanation: “Glass can break and hurt—let’s use plastic/cups with care.”Model the right behaviour and supervise: show how to carry cups carefully, and practice with them when appropriate.

questions.explanation: In this situation, it's important to consider the well-being of your toddler and promote a safe learning environment. Let's evaluate the courses of action: I. Yell at them to be more careful with things made out of glass. This course of action involves raising your voice at the toddler. While it may express your frustration, it's not a constructive way to handle the situation, especially if the child's action was a genuine accident. It may lead to fear and anxiety in the child. II. Punish them to make sure they remember to be extra careful next time. - Punishing a toddler for a genuine accident is generally not recommended. It can create fear and stress, and it's not an effective way to teach them about accidents and safety. It's important to remember that toddlers are still learning and exploring their environment. Given the context of the situation, the better course of action is to: D. Neither I nor II
